refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters, applies deterministic algorithms (physics-based cycle time estimation, energy models, availability calculations), and outputs a structured performance requirement set. Baseline and history managed in a versioned configuration database with audit logging.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Consensus – Performance requirements derived collaboratively across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, each contributing domain-specific estimates (e.g., Vehicle provides mechanical cycle time, Safety Services provides safety margin). A coordinator module aggregates and reconciles estimates into a final baseline.

DP-C: Template-Based Requirement Mapping – Pre-defined requirement templates matched to worksite mission profiles (e.g., 'high-payload earthmoving' template). Templates are parameterized and instantiated with mission inputs. History tracked via template version control.

DP-D: Real-Time Adaptive Derivation – Performance requirements continuously re-derived from live telemetry and operational feedback. Baselines updated dynamically as mission context evolves. History captures continuous evolution rather than discrete snapshots.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with Versioned Configuration Database and Audit Logging

potential_sources: DP-A requires inputs from Vehicle (mechanical parameters, energy models) and Safety Services (safety constraint definitions). These are external dependencies but well-defined interfaces (Telemetry for Vehicle data, SafetyAlert for safety constraints).
DP-A outputs performance requirements that feed into Vehicle operational planning and Safety Services monitoring. This is a one-way dependency (DP-A → downstream consumers), not circular coupling.
Audit logging and versioning are self-contained within the derivation engine; no tight coupling to other modules.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling: it is a data-transformation module with clear input/output contracts. Dependencies are via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). No circular dependencies detected. Decoupling is achieved by treating DP-A as a stateless computation service that consumes structured inputs and produces structured outputs.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured, machine-readable form (e.g., JSON payload from Site Manager interface). Risk: If mission context is unstructured or incomplete, derivation will fail or produce invalid requirements.

ASSUMPTION: Physics-based models for cycle time, energy, and availability are accurate and validated for the NGHE equipment class. Risk: Model inaccuracy will propagate to all derived requirements, affecting downstream planning and safety.

ASSUMPTION: Safety constraint definitions from Safety Services are complete and non-contradictory. Risk: Incomplete or conflicting safety constraints will result in infeasible or unsafe performance requirements.
ASSUMPTION: Audit logging infrastructure (database, versioning system) is available and reliable. Risk: Audit trail loss or corruption will violate traceability and compliance requirements.

RISK: High safety criticality means errors in performance derivation could lead to unsafe equipment operation. Mitigation: Require independent validation of derived requirements by Safety Manager before baseline approval.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound but should be augmented with a mandatory validation gate: derived performance requirements must be reviewed and approved by Safety Manager before being recorded as baseline. This adds a human-in-the-loop safeguard appropriate for high-safety-criticality systems. Additionally, specify that the derivation engine shall log all assumptions and model parameters used in each derivation, enabling post-hoc traceability and root-cause analysis if requirements prove incorrect.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It contains or coordinates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides the unified interface for performance requirement derivation, recording, and management. The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) is realized as a core service within NGHE Solution, with clear interfaces to Vehicle (for mechanical/energy data), Safety Services (for safety constraints), and external stakeholders (Site Manager for mission context input, Maintenance/Quality for history access).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry from Vehicle module providing real-time mechanical parameters (cycle time observations, energy consumption, availability metrics) used to validate and calibrate performance derivation models.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Inbound safety constraint definitions and alerts from Safety Services module, specifying safety margins, operational limits, and hazard mitigations that must be incorporated into derived performance requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound mission context and tasking parameters from Site Manager (worksite terrain, payload, cycle targets, operational policies), which serve as primary inputs to the performance derivation algorithm.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outbound derived performance requirements and baseline metadata (cycle time targets, energy efficiency targets, availability targets, safety margins) to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for operational planning and monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for performance requirement derivation. It is the system-level integration point that coordinates inputs from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The derivation engine is a core system service, not a subsystem-specific function. Containment is very high; diffusion risk is minimal (only risk is if performance derivation logic is duplicated in Vehicle or Safety Services, which should be avoided).

Vehicle module could theoretically contain performance derivation, but this would be poor architectural choice. Vehicle is a physical/operational subsystem; performance derivation is a system-level analytical function. Placing derivation in Vehicle would couple system-level requirements management to vehicle-specific implementation, reducing reusability and creating diffusion risk (e.g., if multiple vehicle types exist, each would need its own derivation logic). Containment score is low.

Teleoperation module is a remote operation subsystem. Performance derivation is not a teleoperation-specific function. Placing derivation in Teleoperation would create artificial coupling and diffusion. Containment score is very low.

Safety Services could contain safety-margin-specific derivation logic, but the broader performance derivation (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) is not safety-specific. Placing the entire derivation engine in Safety Services would conflate safety constraint management with general performance requirement derivation, reducing clarity and creating diffusion. Safety Services should provide safety constraint inputs to NGHE Solution's derivation engine, not contain the engine itself. Containment score is low.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineering / Performance Management team)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) is best realized as a core service module within NGHE Solution, owned by the System Engineering or Performance Management team. This placement ensures: (1) system-level visibility and control over requirement derivation; (2) clear separation of concerns (NGHE Solution owns derivation logic, Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services own their respective operational domains); (3) reusability across multiple vehicle types and mission profiles; (4) centralized audit logging and traceability; (5) alignment with acceptance criteria requiring documented, traceable performance targets at the system level.
containment_risks: RISK: If performance derivation engine is not clearly owned and bounded, derivation logic may diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, leading to inconsistent requirement sets and loss of traceability.

RISK: If NGHE Solution does not provide clear interfaces for mission context input (Tasking) and safety constraint input (SafetyAlert), subsystems may attempt to derive requirements independently, creating conflicts.
RISK: If audit logging is not centralized in NGHE Solution, history of requirement baselines and calibration updates may be fragmented or lost, violating acceptance criteria.
RISK: If the derivation engine is tightly coupled to a specific vehicle type or mission profile, it will not be reusable for future NGHE variants, reducing system flexibility.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish NGHE Solution as the clear owner of the Performance Derivation Engine. Define explicit interface contracts: (1) Tasking interface for mission context input from Site Manager; (2) SafetyAlert interface for safety constraint input from Safety Services; (3) Telemetry interface for vehicle parameter input from Vehicle module; (4) output interface for derived performance requirements to Vehicle and Teleoperation. Implement centralized audit logging within NGHE Solution to capture all derivation events, baseline changes, and calibration updates. Prohibit subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) from independently deriving performance requirements; they shall consume requirements from NGHE Solution's derivation engine.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all aspects of refined FR-NGHE-005: (1) automatic derivation from mission context (DP-A ingests Tasking inputs); (2) recording in traceable baseline (DP-A outputs structured baseline with metadata); (3) managed history with audit trail (DP-A maintains versioned configuration database with audit logging). DP-A is a sound, decoupled solution with low coupling risk. The FR-DP mapping is clear and complete.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): If performance derivation logic is not clearly scoped to NGHE Solution, subsystems may implement local derivation functions, creating inconsistency and loss of traceability. Mitigation: Establish clear ownership and interface contracts in NGHE Solution.
DIFFUSION RISK (Low): If audit logging is not centralized, history may be fragmented across modules. Mitigation: Implement centralized audit logging within NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-A is well-contained within NGHE Solution (containment score 95/100). NGHE Solution is the natural system-level integration point. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) are well-defined and use existing known interfaces. No new interfaces required. Ownership is clear: System Engineering / Performance Management team within NGHE Solution. Containment hypothesis is sound.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-005 is feasible via NGHE Solution's Performance Derivation Engine: (1) Functional verification: confirm that engine correctly ingests mission context, applies derivation algorithms, and outputs performance requirements; (2) Traceability verification: confirm that baseline records include source, timestamp, derivation method, and approver; (3) History verification: confirm that audit log captures all updates with who/what/when/why; (4) Safety verification: confirm that safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented. All verification activities can be performed at NGHE Solution level without requiring subsystem-level testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution module. Sub-FRs are allocated as follows: CR-NGHE-005.1 (ingest mission context) → Tasking interface; CR-NGHE-005.2 (compute requirements) → Performance Derivation Engine; CR-NGHE-005.3 (record baseline) → Configuration Database; CR-NGHE-005.4 (maintain history) → Audit Logging Service; CR-NGHE-005.5 (provide traceability) → Reporting Service. All allocations are within NGHE Solution scope.

recommended_revisions: Revise DP-A to include mandatory validation gate: derived performance requirements must be reviewed and approved by Safety Manager before baseline recording. This adds human-in-the-loop safeguard appropriate for high-safety-criticality systems.

Revise IM assignment to explicitly specify that NGHE Solution shall prohibit subsystems from independently deriving performance requirements. All derivation shall flow through the centralized Performance Derivation Engine.
Revise acceptance criteria to include: 'Audit log shall be immutable and tamper-evident, with cryptographic integrity checks.' This strengthens compliance with traceability and safety requirements.

Revise interface specifications to include: Tasking interface shall carry mission context in structured format (e.g., JSON schema); SafetyAlert interface shall carry safety constraints in structured format; Telemetry interface shall carry vehicle parameters with timestamps and confidence metrics.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-005 has been successfully refined, a sound DP selected, and a clear IM assigned. The FR-DP-IM chain is coherent and passes all gates. However, the refined FR contains five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that require further decomposition to ensure complete coverage and to allocate design responsibility. Specifically: (1) CR-NGHE-005.1 (ingest mission context) requires detailed specification of Tasking interface and mission context schema; (2) CR-NGHE-005.2 (compute requirements) requires detailed specification of derivation algorithms and models; (3) CR-NGHE-005.3 (record baseline) requires detailed specification of baseline data structure and metadata; (4) CR-NGHE-005.4 (maintain history) requires detailed specification of audit logging, versioning, and history query mechanisms; (5) CR-NGHE-005.5 (provide traceability) requires detailed specification of traceability reporting and query interfaces. Each sub-FR should be decomposed to the next level to ensure completeness and to identify any additional DP or IM considerations.
decision.justification_extended: The current level of decomposition is appropriate for system-level architecture, but the sub-FRs are not yet atomic. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will: (1) clarify the detailed design of the Performance Derivation Engine; (2) identify any additional modules or interfaces required; (3) enable detailed verification planning; (4) support detailed design and implementation planning. No revisions to current-level FR, DP, or IM are required; the chain is sound. Proceed with decomposition of sub-FRs.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input via Tasking interface.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms and validated physics-based models.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver, model parameters, assumptions).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why, approval status).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history, with query and export capabilities for Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured, machine-readable form (e.g., JSON payload from Site Manager interface).
Physics-based models for cycle time, energy, and availability are accurate and validated for the NGHE equipment class.
Safety constraint definitions from Safety Services are complete and non-contradictory.
Audit logging infrastructure (database, versioning system) is available and reliable.
Safety Manager is available to review and approve derived performance requirements before baseline recording.
NGHE Solution has authority to establish and enforce interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
Performance requirement derivation is a system-level function, not a subsystem-specific function.
open_risks: Model inaccuracy in physics-based derivation algorithms could propagate to all derived requirements, affecting downstream planning and safety. Mitigation: Require independent validation of models by domain experts; implement sensitivity analysis to quantify impact of model uncertainty.

Incomplete or conflicting safety constraints from Safety Services could result in infeasible or unsafe performance requirements. Mitigation: Implement constraint validation logic in derivation engine; require Safety Manager approval before baseline recording.
Audit trail loss or corruption could violate traceability and compliance requirements. Mitigation: Implement immutable, tamper-evident audit logging with cryptographic integrity checks.

Diffusion of derivation logic across subsystems could create inconsistency and loss of traceability. Mitigation: Establish clear ownership and interface contracts; prohibit subsystems from independently deriving requirements.

High safety criticality means errors in performance derivation could lead to unsafe equipment operation. Mitigation: Implement human-in-the-loop validation gate; require Safety Manager approval; implement independent verification of derived requirements.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of Tasking interface and mission context schema (JSON schema or equivalent).
Validation report for physics-based derivation models (cycle time, energy, availability), including accuracy metrics and uncertainty quantification.
Specification of baseline data structure and metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver, model parameters, assumptions).
Specification of audit logging mechanism (immutable log, tamper-evident, cryptographic integrity checks).
Specification of traceability reporting and query interfaces.
Interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services modules.
Approval process and criteria for Safety Manager review of derived performance requirements.
Test plan for Performance Derivation Engine, including unit tests, integration tests, and system-level verification.
